why_wrong: Always Resettable Toggle: keeps the file compilable but forces the observable output path low, so it can pass include/instance smoke checks while failing the required behavior to A rising rst edge must immediately clear q to 0.
Always Resettable Toggle: keeps the event structure but inverts the select/data decision, so simple reset wiring may look valid while the Use edge-triggered always with async reset behavior is wrong.
Always Resettable Toggle: preserves clocked behavior but drops reset handling, so post-reset or mid-run reset checks fail even though non-reset toggles may still occur.
Always Resettable Toggle: responds on the wrong event edge, so static wiring may look plausible but the timed hidden samples disagree with the required edge behavior.
Always Resettable Toggle: attenuates the computed output level, so transitions remain visible but amplitude-sensitive checks reject the low-scale response.
